vocab.meaning: sự kiểm tra chớp nhoáng/sơ sài
vocab.example: I usually give myself a cursory inspection in the mirror before leaving for work just to ensure my collar isn't tucked in.

vocab.meaning: ám ảnh/chăm chú vào những khuyết điểm tự cảm thấy
vocab.example: I try not to spend too much time looking at my reflection because I tend to fixate on perceived flaws that others wouldn't even notice.
Practicing my speech in front of a mirror helps me project an air of confidence before a major presentation.

vocab.meaning: tự trấn an về diện mạo của mình
vocab.example: I find myself glancing at shop windows occasionally just to reassure myself of my appearance after a long commute.
A mirror is essential for me to adjust my attire and make sure my tie is perfectly straight.

vocab.word: preen in front of the glass
vocab.meaning: tỉa tót/chăm chút diện mạo trước gương
vocab.example: I wouldn't say I'm vain, but I do spend a few minutes each morning to preen in front of the glass.

vocab.meaning: hình ảnh bản thân bị bóp méo/sai lệch
vocab.example: Spending excessive time analyzing one's reflection can sometimes lead to a distorted self-image.

vocab.meaning: duy trì một hình ảnh bóng bẩy/chỉn chu
vocab.example: As a public speaker, looking in the mirror frequently is a necessity to maintain a polished image throughout the day.

vocab.meaning: kiểm tra nhanh hình ảnh phản chiếu của mình
vocab.example: Whenever I pass a mirror in a hallway, I might briefly check my reflection out of habit.

vocab.meaning: khoảnh khắc tự suy ngẫm (cả nghĩa đen và nghĩa bóng)
vocab.example: To me, the mirror provides a self-reflective moment where I can gather my thoughts while brushing my teeth.
